# Snowflake Connector - Full Documentation

# Snowflake Connector 1.4

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Snowflake (Snowflake Connector) enables you to connect with your Snowflake
instance to load data, run queries in Snowflake tables, and sync data with external business
applications.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, see the Snowflake Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Snowflake API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the Snowflake target resource
- Anypoint Platform
- Anypoint Studio version 7.5 or later

## Common Use Cases for the Connector

These are some common use cases for Snowflake Connector:

- Load bulk or continuous data into your Snowflake instance.
- Run queries to insert, delete, update, and merge data in Snowflake tables.
- Sync data:
  - Keep unstructured data (files) in an external data location in sync with unstructured data in
    Snowflake, when the external data location is the source of truth.
  - Keep unstructured data (files) in an external data location in sync with unstructured data in
    Snowflake, when Snowflake is the source of truth.
  - Keep structured data (tables) in an external data location in sync with Snowflake (in tables),
    when the external data location is the source of truth.
  - Keep structured data (tables) in an external data location in sync with Snowflake (in tables),
    when Snowflake is the source of truth.

## Connection Types

Use one of the following Snowflake connection types:

- Key-pair Snowflake Connection  
  Enables you to connect to Snowflake using encrypted or unencrypted private keys.
- Basic Snowflake Connection (To be deprecated)  
  Enables you to connect to Snowflake via user and password.

For information about configuring authentication types, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Snowflake Connector](snowflake-connector-studio).

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, you are ready to create an app and configure the connector
using [Anypoint Studio](snowflake-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Snowflake Connector Reference](snowflake-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Snowflake Connector 1.4 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following example returns all rows of a table based on a watermark.

This example uses variables for some field values. You can either:

- Replace the variables with their values in the code.
- Provide the values for each variable in a properties file and then refer to that file from the
  connector configuration.

If you donât know how to use a properties file, refer to
[Configuring Property Placeholders](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-to-configure).

Before you try the examples, access Anypoint Studio (Studio) and verify that the **Mule Palette**
view displays entries for Snowflake Connector. If not, follow the instructions in
[Add the Connector to Your Mule Project](snowflake-connector-studio#add-connector-to-project).

## Return All Rows of a Table

This example shows how to use the **On Table Row** source to return rows that contain columns with a
greater watermark value than that of the last watermark processed.

The following screenshot shows the app flow for this example:

![Studio Flow for the On Table Row source](_images/listener-flow.png)

### Add the On Table Row Source

The **On Table Row** source selects from a table at a regular interval and generates one message per
obtained row.

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Snowflake** and drag the **On Table Row** source to the
    canvas.
2.  In the Search properties window, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to
    add a global element.
3.  Enter the following values:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><p><code>Snowflake_Config</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Table</p></td><td><p><code>USERS</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Watermark Column</p></td><td><p><code>CREATION_DATE</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id Column</p></td><td><p><code>ID</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Since</p></td><td><p><code>2021-12-14 11:00:00.000</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Add the Transform Message Component

The **Transform Message** component outputs the rows of the table in JSON format.

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag the **Transform Message** component to
    the right of **On Table Row**.
2.  Click the **Transform Message** component and set the output format to `application/json`.

### Add the Logger Component

The **Logger** component logs the query result to the console.

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag the **Logger** component to the right of
    **Transform Message**.
2.  Enter the following values:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p><code>Logger</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>payload</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Level</p></td><td><p><code>INFO (Default)</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:db="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/db" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:snowflake="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/snowflake"
	xmlns:salesforce="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/snowflake http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/snowflake/current/mule-snowflake.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/db http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/db/current/mule-db.xsd">

	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties"/>

	<snowflake:snowflake-config name="Snowflake_Config" >
		<snowflake:snowflake-connection accountName="${config.accountName}" warehouse="${config.warehouse}" database="${config.database}" schema="${config.schema}" user="${config.user}" password="${config.password}" role="${config.role}"/>
	</snowflake:snowflake-config>
	<snowflake:snowflake-config name="keyPairWithPasswordConfig" >
        	<snowflake:keypair-connection user="${config.user}"  accountName="${config.accountName}" warehouse="${config.warehouse}" database="${config.database}" schema="${config.schema}" privateKeyFile="${app.home}/rsa_key_encrypted.p8" privateKeyPassword="${config.user.keypair.password}" role="${config.role}"/>
    </snowflake:snowflake-config>
	<flow name="listener-flow" >
		<snowflake:row-listener doc:name="Row Listener" config-ref="Snowflake_Config" table="USERS" watermarkColumn="CREATION_DATE" idColumn="ID" since="2021-12-14 11:00:00.000">
			<scheduling-strategy >
				<fixed-frequency />
			</scheduling-strategy>
		</snowflake:row-listener>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" message="#[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  The terminal will display the new messages for this source.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Configuring FIPS 140-3 for Snowflake Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Snowflake Connector supports FIPS 140-3 compliant cryptographic operations through integration with
BouncyCastle FIPS (BCFIPS) security provider. Learn how to configure the connector for
FIPS-compliant environments.

## Before You Begin

Make sure you meet these prerequisites for the configuration:

- Mule Runtime 4.10.0 or later
- Snowflake Connector version 1.4.0 or later
- BouncyCastle FIPS libraries v2 or later
- Java 17 or later with FIPS-capable JVM
- Snowflake JDBC Driver - FIPS Edition (required for FIPS environments)

> [!WARNING] FIPS-Certified JDBC driver required:
>
> Snowflake provides a separate FIPS-certified JDBC driver specifically designed for FIPS 140-3
> compliant environments. If you operate in a FIPS environment, you must use the FIPS-certified
> driver instead of the standard Snowflake JDBC driver.
>
> - Standard driver  
>   `snowflake-jdbc-<version>.jar` (for non-FIPS environments)
> - FIPS driver  
>   `snowflake-jdbc-fips-<version>.jar` (for FIPS environments)
>
> The FIPS driver is built with FIPS-certified cryptographic libraries and is validated for use in
> regulated environments. Using the standard driver in a FIPS environment doesnât meet compliance
> requirements.
>
> For more information on obtaining the FIPS-certified driver, see the
> [Snowflake JDBC Driver documentation](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/jdbc/jdbc).

## Understanding FIPS Mode Detection

Snowflake Connector automatically detects FIPS mode by checking the `mule.security.model` system
property. When this property is set to a value starting with `fips140` (for example, `fips140-3`),
the connector operates in FIPS mode with enhanced security controls.

Example:

```bash
-Dmule.security.model=fips140-3
```

## Configure BCFIPS with the Snowflake Connector

Configure BCFIPS using one of these options:

- [Configure BCFIPS in Java Security](#configure-bcfips-in-java-security)
- [Enable Auto-Registration](#enable-auto-registration)

> [!NOTE] FIPS approved algorithms only:
>
> Regardless of which configuration option you choose, you must configure the system property
> `org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only=true` to ensure that the BCFIPS security provider operates
> exclusively with FIPS-approved algorithms.
>
> The connector doesnât set this property automatically. This is a JVM-wide configuration that
> affects all applications and processes running in the JVM. The system administrator must set this
> property manually or through your Mule runtime configuration.
>
> Example:
>
> ```bash
> -Dorg.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only=true
> ```
>
> Without this property, BCFIPS may allow non-FIPS-approved algorithms, which could violate
> compliance requirements in regulated environments.

### Configure BCFIPS in Java Security (Recommended)

Use this option for production environments to:

- Manage security configuration from a central location
- Apply consistently across all applications
- Avoid runtime modifications to security infrastructure
- Follow enterprise security governance practices

Follow these steps:

1.  Locate your Java security configuration file:

    $JAVA_HOME/jre/lib/security/java.security

2.  Add the BCFIPS provider to the security provider list. Insert it at an appropriate position
    (typically position 1 or 2):

    ```properties
    security.provider.1=org.bouncycastle.jcajce.provider.BouncyCastleFipsProvider
    security.provider.2=sun.security.provider.Sun
    # ... other providers
    ```

3.  Make sure the BCFIPS JAR is available in the JVM classpath or the Mule applicationâs shared
    libraries.
4.  Restart your Mule runtime instance.

### Enable Auto-Registration

Use this option for environments where modifying `java.security` is not feasible, the connector can
automatically register BCFIPS at runtime.

> [!WARNING] Important security considerations:
>
> - Modifies the JVM-wide security provider configuration
> - May conflict with existing security providers
> - Requires explicit approval from your security or compliance team
> - Should only be used when
>   [configuring BCFIPS in Java Security](#configure-bcfips-in-java-security) is not possible

Follow these steps:

1.  Set the auto-registration system property:

    ```bash
    -Dmule.snowflake.fips.bcfips.autoregister=true
    ```

2.  Make sure FIPS mode is enabled:

    ```bash
    -Dmule.security.model=fips140-3
    ```

3.  The connector automatically registers BCFIPS when establishing the first connection.

Example Mule runtime startup:

```bash
java -Dmule.security.model=fips140-3 \
     -Dmule.snowflake.fips.bcfips.autoregister=true \
     -Dorg.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only=true \
     -jar mule-standalone.jar
```

## Understanding Standard (Non-FIPS) Mode

In standard mode (when `mule.security.model` isnât set or doesnât start with `fips140`), the
connector automatically handles BCFIPS registration without requiring additional configuration. This
ensures seamless operation for non-regulated environments.

## System Properties Reference

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Property</th><th>Description</th><th>Values</th><th>Default</th><th>Scope</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>mule.security.model</code></p></td><td><p>Enables FIPS mode detection</p></td><td><p><code>fips140-3</code>, <code>fips140-2</code>, or any value starting with <code>fips140</code></p></td><td><p>Not set (standard mode)</p></td><td><p>Connector</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>mule.snowflake.fips.bcfips.autoregister</code></p></td><td><p>Enables automatic BCFIPS registration in FIPS mode</p></td><td><p><code>true</code>, <code>false</code></p></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td><td><p>Connector</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only</code></p></td><td><p><strong>REQUIRED for FIPS compliance.</strong> Restricts BCFIPS to use only FIPS-approved algorithms</p></td><td><p><code>true</code>, <code>false</code></p></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td><td><p><strong>JVM-wide</strong></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!WARNING] The `org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only` property affects the entire JVM and all
> applications running in it. This property isnât set by the connector and the system administrator
> must configure it manually.

## Migrate Existing Deployments

You need to assess the current configuration and choose the appropriate configuration method.

### Non-FIPS Environments

No action required. The connector maintains full backward compatibility and automatically handles
BCFIPS registration.

### For FIPS Environments

1.  Assess the current configuration:
    - Verify if BCFIPS is already configured in `java.security`
    - Check for existing BouncyCastle provider registrations
    - Identify the Snowflake JDBC driver in use (standard versus FIPS-certified)

2.  Choose the configuration method:
    - Recommended: [Configure BCFIPS in `java.security`](#configure-bcfips-in-java-security)
    - Alternative: [Enable auto-registration](#enable-auto-registration)

3.  Configure FIPS-certified components:
    - Replace standard Snowflake JDBC driver with FIPS-certified driver (`snowflake-jdbc-fips`)
    - Make sure BouncyCastle FIPS libraries version 2.x are available

4.  Update system properties:
    - Add `-Dmule.security.model=fips140-3` (enables FIPS mode detection)
    - Add `-Dorg.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only=true` (Required for FIPS compliance)
    - If using auto-registration, add `-Dmule.snowflake.fips.bcfips.autoregister=true`

5.  Test in Non-Production:
    - Deploy to development or staging environment
    - Verify connectivity and operations
    - Review logs for any security-related warnings

6.  Production deployment:
    - Schedule maintenance window
    - Deploy configuration changes
    - Monitor logs for successful FIPS initialization
    - Validate all Snowflake operations

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Snowflake Connector](snowflake-connector-studio)
- [Snowflake Connector Reference](snowflake-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Snowflake Connector 1.4 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

## Snowflake Configuration

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#key-pair-connection">Key-pair Connection</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#snowflake-config_snowflake-connection">Basic Snowflake Connection (To be deprecated)</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Key-pair Connection

For further details on how to configure key-pair authentication, refer to
[configure key-pair authentication](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/key-pair-auth#configuring-key-pair-authentication)
Snowflake documentation.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Pooling Profile</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SnowflakeDbPoolingProfile">Snowflake Db Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures database connection pooling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Account Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Account name provided when registering for the Snowflake account. The account name is in the <code>&lt;OrgId&gt;.&lt;AccountId&gt;</code> format. Specify the account name with a cloud region or cloud name, for example, <code>&lt;OrgId&gt;.&lt;AccountId&gt;.&lt;CloudRegion&gt;</code> or <code>&lt;OrgId&gt;.&lt;AccountId&gt;.&lt;CloudRegion&gt;.&lt;CloudName&gt;</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Warehouse</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the Snowflake warehouse to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Database</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Snowflake database to connect to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Schema</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Snowflake database schema to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Snowflake database username used to initialize the session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Key File Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Private key path used to authenticate the user.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password to decrypt an encrypted private key. Only for encrypted private keys.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Role</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Role assigned to the user.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Basic Snowflake Connection (To be deprecated)

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Pooling Profile</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SnowflakeDbPoolingProfile">Snowflake Db Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Provides a way to configure database connection pooling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Account Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Account name that was provided when registering for the Snowflake account. The account name is in the <code>&lt;OrgId&gt;.&lt;AccountId&gt;</code> format. You can also specify the account name with a cloud region or cloud name, for example, <code>&lt;OrgId&gt;.&lt;AccountId&gt;.&lt;CloudRegion&gt;</code> or <code>&lt;OrgId&gt;.&lt;AccountId&gt;.&lt;CloudRegion&gt;.&lt;CloudName&gt;</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Warehouse</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the Snowflake warehouse to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Database</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Snowflake database to connect to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Schema</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Snowflake database schema to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Snowflake database username used to initialize the session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Snowflake database password used to authenticate the user.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Role</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Role assigned to the user.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Create Stage](#createStage)
- [Create Task](#createTask)
- [Delete](#delete)
- [Execute Ddl](#executeDdl)
- [Execute Script](#executeScript)
- [Insert](#insert)
- [Insert Multi Table](#insertMultiTable)
- [Merge](#merge)
- [Select](#select)
- [Stored Procedure](#storedProcedure)
- [Update](#update)

## Associated Sources

- [On Table Row](#Listener)

## Operations

### Bulk Delete

`<snowflake:bulk-delete>`

Enables execution of a single delete statement at various times using different parameter bindings.
Using a single database statement has performance advantages compared to executing a single
operation at various times.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A list of maps in which every list item represents a row to delete. The map contains the parameter names as keys and the value the parameter is bound to.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement. No timeout is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a result set. This property is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value of 10 is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any result set object generated by this message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SQL String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text of the SQL query to execute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameter Types</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optionally specifies the type of one or more of the parameters in the query. If provided, youâre not required to reference all of the parameters, but you canât reference a parameter that isnât present in the input values.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Bulk Insert

`<snowflake:bulk-insert>`

Enables execution of a single insert statement at various times using different parameter bindings.
Using a single database statement has performance advantages compared to executing a single update
operation at various times.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A list of maps in which every list item represents a row to insert. The map contains the parameter names as keys and the value the parameter is bound to.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement. No timeout is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a result set. This property is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value of 10 is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any result set object generated by the message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SQL String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text of the SQL query to execute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameter Types</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Allows you to optionally specify the type of one or more of the parameters in the query. If provided, youâre not required to reference all of the parameters, but you canât reference a parameter that isnât present in the input values.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Bulk Update

`<snowflake:bulk-update>`

Enables execution of a single update statement at various times using different parameter bindings.
Using a single database statement has performance advantages compared to executing a single update
operation at various times.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A list of maps in which every list item represents a row to update. The map contains the parameter names as keys and the value the parameter is bound to.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of joining action that operations can take regarding transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement. No timeout is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a result set. This property is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value (<code>10</code>) is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any result set object generated by the message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SQL String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text of the SQL query to execute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameter Types</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables you to optionally specify the type of one or more of the parameters in the query. If provided, youâre not required to reference all of the parameters, but you canât reference a parameter that isnât present in the input values.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Copy Into Location

`<snowflake:copy-into-location>`

Unloads data from a table (or query) into one or more files.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Location</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#SnowflakeLocation">Snowflake Location</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ExternalLocation">External Location</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the internal or external location where the files containing the data to load are staged.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Table Query Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#table">Table</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#query">Query</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the table or query used as the source of the data to unload.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#DefinitionByNameForCopyIntoLocation">Definition By Name For Copy Into Location</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#DefinitionByTypeForCopyIntoLocation">Definition By Type For Copy Into Location</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the format of the data files to load.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Include Header</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to include the table column headings in the output files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Overwrite</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean that specifies whether the COPY command overwrites existing files with matching names in the location where the files are stored.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Single</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean that specifies whether to generate a single file or multiple files. If <code>false</code>, a filename prefix must be included in the path.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max File Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number (&gt; 0) that specifies the upper size limit (in bytes) of each file to generate in parallel per thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>16000000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Include Query Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean that specifies whether to uniquely identify unloaded files by including a universally unique identifier (UUID) in the filenames of unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Detailed Output</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to load data into columns in the target table that match corresponding columns represented in the data.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Copy Into Table

`<snowflake:copy-into-table>`

Loads data from staged files to an existing table. The files must already be staged in one of the
following locations:

- Named internal stage (or table/user stage). Files can be staged using the PUT command.
- Named external stage that references an external location (Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, or
  Microsoft Azure).
- External location (Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, or Microsoft Azure).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination Table Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the name of the table into which data is loaded.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Location</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#SnowflakeLocation">Snowflake Location</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ExternalLocation">External Location</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the internal or external location where the files containing the data to be loaded are staged.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Files</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of one or more files to load from a staged internal or external location.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pattern</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the file names or paths to match based on a regex pattern.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#DefinitionByNameForCopyIntoTable">Definition By Name For Copy Into Table</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#DefinitionByTypeForCopyIntoTable">Definition By Type For Copy Into Table</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the format of the data files to load.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Validation Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#ValidationModeReturnErrors">Validation Mode Return Errors</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ValidationModeReturnMaxRows">Validation Mode Return Max Rows</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies that the load operation should only validate data and return the results based on validation options rather than loading data into the specified table.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>On Error</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Action to perform when an error is seen while loading data from a file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Size Limit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size in bytes of data to load with a given copy statement.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Purge</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to remove the data files from the stage automatically after the data is loaded successfully.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Return Failed Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to return only files that have failed to load in the statement result.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Match By Column Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CASE_SENSITIVE</p></li><li><p>CASE_INSENSITIVE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to load data into columns in the target table that match corresponding columns represented in the data.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enforce Length</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to truncate text strings that exceed the target column length.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Truncate Columns</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to truncate text strings that exceed the target column length.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Force</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, loads all files, regardless of whether theyâve been loaded previously and have not changed since they were loaded.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Load Uncertain Files</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies to load files for which the load status is unknown.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Copy Into Table With Transformation

`<snowflake:copy-into-table-with-transformation>`

Loads data from staged files to an existing table. The files must already be staged in one of the
following locations:

- Named internal stage (or table/user stage). Files can be staged using the PUT command.
- Named external stage that references an external location (Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, or
  Microsoft Azure).
- External location (Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, or Microsoft Azure).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination Table Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the name of the table into which data is loaded.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Column Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies an explicit list of table columns (separated by commas) into which you want to insert data.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transformation Select SQL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies an explicit set of fields or columns (separated by commas) to load from the staged data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Stage Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the internal or external location where the files containing the data to be loaded are staged.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Stage Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies an optional alias for the FROM value (for example <code>d</code> in <code>COPY INTO t1 (c1) FROM (SELECT d.$1 FROM @mystage/file1.csv.gz d));</code>).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Files</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of one or more files to load from a staged internal or external location.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pattern</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the file names or paths to match based on a regular expression pattern.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#DefinitionByNameForCopyIntoTable">Definition By Name For Copy Into Table</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#DefinitionByTypeForCopyIntoTable">Definition By Type For Copy Into Table</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the format of the data files to load.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>On Error</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Action to perform when an error is seen while loading data from a file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Size Limit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size in bytes of data to be loaded with a given copy statement.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Purge</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to remove the data files from the stage automatically after the data is loaded successfully.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Return Failed Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to return only files that have failed to load in the statement result.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Match By Column Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CASE_SENSITIVE</p></li><li><p>CASE_INSENSITIVE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to load data into columns in the target table that match corresponding columns represented in the data.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enforce Length</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to truncate text strings that exceed the target column length.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Truncate Columns</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to truncate text strings that exceed the target column length.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Force</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, loads all files, regardless of whether theyâve been loaded previously and have not changed since they were loaded.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Load Uncertain Files</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies to load files for which the load status is unknown.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Create Pipe

`<snowflake:create-pipe>`

Creates a new pipe in the system for defining the COPY INTO <table> statement used by Snowpipe to
load data from an ingestion queue into tables.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pipe Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the identifier for the pipe; must be unique for the schema in which the pipe is created.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Copy Statement</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the statement used to load data from queued files into a Snowflake table. This statement serves as the text/definition for the pipe and is displayed in the SHOW PIPES output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replace Existing Pipe</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies if an already existing pipe should be overwritten by the one being created.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>If Not Exists</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies if the pipe should be created if there isnât already an existing pipe with the same name.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Ingest</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#true">True</a></p></li><li><p>false</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether data should be automatically loaded from the specified external stage and optional path when event notifications are received from a configured message service.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Comment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a comment for the pipe.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Create Stage

`<snowflake:create-stage>`

Creates a new named stage to use for loading data from files into Snowflake tables and unloading
data from tables into files.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Stage Location</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#InternalStageLocation">Internal Stage Location</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ExternalStageLocation">External Stage Location</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Snowflake internal or external location to create the stage.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>If Not Exists</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies if the stage should not be created if there is already an existing stage with the same name.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replace Already Existing Stage</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies if an already existing stage should be overwritten by the one being created.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Is Temporary</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether or not the stage being created is temporary.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#FileFormatDefinitionByName">File Format Definition By Name</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#FileFormatDefinitionByTypeOptions">File Format Definition By Type Options</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the type of files for the stage.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Comment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a comment for the stage.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>On Error</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Action to perform when an error is seen while loading data from a file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Size Limit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size in bytes of data to load with a given copy statement.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Purge</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to remove the data files from the stage automatically after the data is loaded successfully.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Return Failed Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to return only files that have failed to load in the statement result.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Match By Column Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CASE_SENSITIVE</p></li><li><p>CASE_INSENSITIVE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to load data into columns in the target table that match corresponding columns represented in the data.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enforce Length</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to truncate text strings that exceed the target column length.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Truncate Columns</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to truncate text strings that exceed the target column length.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Force Load</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, loads all files, regardless of whether theyâve been loaded previously and have not changed since they were loaded.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Create Task

`<snowflake:create-task>`

Creates a new named external stage to use for:

- Loading data from files into Snowflake tables
- Unloading data from tables into files

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Task Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the name of the task; must be unique for the schema in which the task is created.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Warehouse Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the name of the warehouse.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sql</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the query to run.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replace Existing Task</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies if an existing task should be overwritten by the one being created.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>If Not Exists</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies if the task should be created if there isnât already an existing task with the same name.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Session Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the session parameters to set for the session when the task runs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Run Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a time limit in milliseconds on a single task run before it times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Copy Grants</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether access permissions from the original task should be retained when cloning or replacing an existing task.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Comment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a comment for the task.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>After Task</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the name of a predecessor task which would trigger the created task whenever the predecessor task is run.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>When</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SQL expression to determine whether a task should run.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Schedule Option Cron Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ScheduleOptionCronExpression">Schedule Option Cron Expression</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a cron expression and time zone for periodically running the task. Supports a subset of standard cron utility syntax.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Schedule Option Time Interval</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ScheduleOptionTimeInterval">Schedule Option Time Interval</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies an interval (in minutes) of wait time inserted between runs of the task. Accepts positive integers only.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Delete

`<snowflake:delete>`

Removes data from a table using an optional WHERE clause and/or additional tables.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement.
