No timeout is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a result set. This property is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value of 10 is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any result set object generated by the message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SQL String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text of the SQL query to execute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameter Types</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optionally specify the type of one or more of the parameters in the query. If provided, youâre not required to reference all of the parameters, but you canât reference a parameter that isnât present in the input values.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map in which keys are the name of an input parameter to set on the JDBC prepared statement. Reference each parameter in the SQL text using a colon as the prefix, for example where <code>ID = :myParamName</code>. The mapâs values will contain the actual assignation for each parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Execute Ddl

`<snowflake:execute-ddl>`

Data Definition Language (DDL) commands are used to create, manipulate, and modify objects in
Snowflake, such as users, virtual warehouses, databases, schemas, tables, views, columns, functions,
and stored procedures.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SQL String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SQL DDL statement to execute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement. No timeout is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a result set. This property is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value of 10 is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any result set object generated by the message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Execute Script

`<snowflake:execute-script>`

Executes a SQL script in one single database statement. The script is executed as provided by the
user, without any parameter binding.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SQL Query Text</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Script Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement. No timeout is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a result set. This property is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value of 10 is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any result set object generated by the message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY

### Insert

`<snowflake:insert>`

Updates a table by inserting one or more rows into the table. The values inserted into each column
in the table can be explicitly specified or they can be the results of a query.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement. No timeout is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a result set. This property is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value of 10 is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any result set object generated by the message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SQL String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text of the SQL query to execute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameter Types</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optionally specify the type of one or more of the parameters in the query. If provided, youâre not required to reference all of the parameters, but you canât reference a parameter that isnât present in the input values.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map in which keys are the name of an input parameter to set on the JDBC prepared statement. Reference each parameter in the SQL text using a colon as the prefix, for example where <code>ID = :myParamName</code>. The mapâs values will contain the actual assignation for each parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Generate Keys</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates when to make auto-generated keys available for retrieval.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Generated Keys Column Indexes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of column indexes that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Generated Keys Column Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of column names that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#StatementResult">Statement Result</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY

### Insert Multi Table

`<snowflake:insert-multi-table>`

Updates multiple tables by inserting one or more rows with column values (from a query) into the
tables. Supports both unconditional and conditional inserts.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insert Into Multi Table Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Conditional">Conditional</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Unconditional">Unconditional</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY

### Merge

`<snowflake:merge>`

Inserts, updates, and deletes values in a table based on values in a second table or a subquery.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Table</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the table to merge.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the table or subquery to join with the target table.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Join Expr</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the expression on which to join the target table and source.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Match Clauses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#NotMatchedInsertClause">Not Matched Insert Clause</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#MatchedUpdateClause">Matched Update Clause</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#MatchedDeleteClause">Matched Delete Clause</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of clauses and actions to perform when a match occurs or does not occur.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Select

`<snowflake:select>`

SELECT can be used in both a statement and a clause within a SELECT statement. As a statement, the
SELECT statement is the most commonly executed SQL statement; it queries the database and retrieves
a set of rows. As a clause, SELECT defines the set of columns returned by a query.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement. No timeout is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a result set. This property is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value of 10 is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any result set object generated by the message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SQL String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text of the SQL query to execute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameter Types</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optionally specify the type of one or more of the parameters in the query. If provided, youâre not required to reference all of the parameters, but you canât reference a parameter that isnât present in the input values.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map in which keys are the name of an input parameter to set on the JDBC prepared statement. Reference each parameter in the SQL text using a colon as the prefix, for example where <code>ID = :myParamName</code>. The mapâs values will contain the actual assignation for each parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Stored Procedure

`<snowflake:stored-procedure>`

Invokes a stored procedure on the database. When the stored procedure returns one or more result set
instances, streaming is automatically applied to avoid preemptive consumption of such results, which
can lead to performance and memory issues.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement. No timeout is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a result set. This property is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value of 10 is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any result set object generated by the message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SQL String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text of the SQL query to execute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameter Types</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optionally specify the type of one or more of the parameters in the query. If provided, youâre not required to reference all of the parameters, but you canât reference a parameter that isnât present in the input values.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map in which keys are the name of an input parameter to set on the JDBC prepared statement. Reference each parameter in the SQL text using a colon as the prefix, for example where <code>ID = :myParamName</code>. The mapâs values will contain the actual assignation for each parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Generate Keys</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates when to make auto-generated keys available for retrieval.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Generated Keys Column Indexes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of column indexes that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Generated Keys Column Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of column names that indicates which auto-generated keys should be made available for retrieval.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

### Update

`<snowflake:update>`

Updates specified rows in the target table with new values.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can perform for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement. No timeout is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a resultSet. This property is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value of 10 is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any Result set object generated by the message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SQL String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text of the SQL query to execute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameter Types</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optionally specify the type of one or more of the parameters in the query. If provided, youâre not required to reference all of the parameters, but you canât reference a parameter thatâs not present in the input values.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map in which keys are the name of an input parameter to set on the JDBC prepared statement. Reference each parameter in the SQL text using a colon as the prefix, for example where <code>ID = :myParamName</code>. The mapâs values will contain the actual assignation for each parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Generate Keys</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates when to make auto-generated keys available for retrieval.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Generated Keys Column Indexes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of column indexes that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Generated Keys Column Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of column names that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#StatementResult">Statement Result</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

#### Throws

- SNOWFLAKE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNOWFLAKE:CONNECTIVITY
- SNOWFLAKE:NOT_FOUND
- SNOWFLAKE:QUERY_EXECUTION
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SNOWFLAKE:INVALID_INPUT
- SNOWFLAKE:CANNOT_LOAD_DRIVER
- SNOWFLAKE:UNKNOWN_SNOWFLAKE_ERROR
- SNOWFLAKE:DATABASE

## Sources

### On Table Row

`<snowflake:listener>`

Returns all rows of a table based on a watermark.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_BEGIN</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Type of joining action that sources can perform for transactions.</p></td><td><p>NONE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transaction Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>LOCAL</p></li><li><p>XA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Type of transaction to create. Availability depends on the Mule runtime version.</p></td><td><p>LOCAL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scheduling Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p>RedeliveryPolicy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Table</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the table to select rows from.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Watermark Column</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the column to use for the watermark. Values taken from this column are used to filter the contents of the next poll, so that rows only with a greater watermark value are processed.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id Column</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the column to consider as the row ID. If provided, this parameter ensures the same row is not processed twice by concurrent polls.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the initial amount of registers returned in the results.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before the JDBC driver attempts to cancel a running statement. No timeout is used by default.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates how many rows to fetch from the database when rows are read from a result set. This parameter is required when streaming is true, in which case a default value (<code>10</code>) is used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the limit for the maximum number of rows that any ResultSet object generated by this message processor can contain for the given number. If the limit is exceeded, the excess rows are silently dropped.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Snowflake Configuration](#snowflake-config)

## Types

### Snowflake Db Pooling Profile

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Pool Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of connections a pool maintains at any given time</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Min Pool Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Minimum number of connections a pool maintains at any given time</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Acquire Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines how many connections at a time to try to acquire when the pool is exhausted</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prepared Statement Cache Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines how many statements are cached per pooled connection. Setting this to zero will disable statement caching</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Wait</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of time a client trying to obtain a connection waits for it to be acquired when the pool is exhausted. Zero (default) means wait indefinitely</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Wait Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A #maxWait.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines how many seconds a Connection can remain pooled but unused before being discarded. Zero means idle connections never expire.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>10800</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Additional Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Reconnection strategy to use.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector operation source or operation
attempts to reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before
Mule considers it eligible for expiration.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameter Type

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the input parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type Classifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TypeClassifier">Type Classifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Type Classifier

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BIT</p></li><li><p>TINYINT</p></li><li><p>SMALLINT</p></li><li><p>INTEGER</p></li><li><p>BIGINT</p></li><li><p>FLOAT</p></li><li><p>REAL</p></li><li><p>DOUBLE</p></li><li><p>NUMERIC</p></li><li><p>DECIMAL</p></li><li><p>CHAR</p></li><li><p>VARCHAR</p></li><li><p>LONGVARCHAR</p></li><li><p>DATE</p></li><li><p>TIME</p></li><li><p>TIMESTAMP</p></li><li><p>BINARY</p></li><li><p>VARBINARY</p></li><li><p>LONGVARBINARY</p></li><li><p>NULL</p></li><li><p>OTHER</p></li><li><p>JAVA_OBJECT</p></li><li><p>DISTINCT</p></li><li><p>STRUCT</p></li><li><p>ARRAY</p></li><li><p>BLOB</p></li><li><p>CLOB</p></li><li><p>REF</p></li><li><p>DATALINK</p></li><li><p>BOOLEAN</p></li><li><p>ROWID</p></li><li><p>NCHAR</p></li><li><p>NVARCHAR</p></li><li><p>LONGNVARCHAR</p></li><li><p>NCLOB</p></li><li><p>SQLXML</p></li><li><p>UNKNOWN</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Schedule Option Cron Expression

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Cron Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a cron expression that identifies when to repeat runs of the task.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Zone</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the time zone to use for the cron expression.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Schedule Option Time Interval

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Time Interval In Minutes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a time interval in minutes to wait between periodic runs of the task. Also supports num M syntax.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Statement Result

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Affected Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Generated Keys</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of instances to initially keep in memory to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Objects</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of instances to keep in memory. If more than that is required, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

When streaming in this mode, Mule does not use the disk to buffer the contents. If you exceed the
buffer size, the message fails.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of memory to allocate to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory to use. If more than what is specified is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Avro

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>GZIP</p></li><li><p>BZ2</p></li><li><p>BROTLI</p></li><li><p>ZSTD</p></li><li><p>DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>RAW_DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trim Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first value to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### File Format Options CSV

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>GZIP</p></li><li><p>BZ2</p></li><li><p>BROTLI</p></li><li><p>ZSTD</p></li><li><p>DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>RAW_DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Record Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more single-byte or multibyte characters that separate records in an input file or unloaded file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Field Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more single-byte or multibyte characters that separate records in an input file or unloaded file.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>,</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Extension</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Skip Header Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Skip Blank Lines</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to skip any blank lines encountered in data, instead of resulting in an end-of-record error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Date Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Binary Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HEX</p></li><li><p>BASE64</p></li><li><p>UTF8</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HEX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Escape Character</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Escape Unenclosed Field Character</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a single character used as the escape character for unenclosed field values only.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trim Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Field Optionally Enclosed By</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first one to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error On Column Count Mismatch</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to generate a parsing error if the number of delimited columns (i.e. fields) in an input file does not match the number of columns in the corresponding table.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replace Invalid Characters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to replace invalid UTF-8 characters with a default Unicode character instead of resulting in an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Validate UTF8</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Empty Field As Null</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When set to <code>false</code>, allows empty strings to be loaded in tables without enclosing the field values in quotes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Skip Byte Order Mark</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to skip any byte order mark information from input files so that they wouldnât cause errors or be merged into the first table column.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BIG5</p></li><li><p>EUCJP</p></li><li><p>EUCKR</p></li><li><p>GB18030</p></li><li><p>IBM420</p></li><li><p>IBM424</p></li><li><p>ISO2022CN</p></li><li><p>ISO2022JP</p></li><li><p>ISO2022KR</p></li><li><p>ISO88591</p></li><li><p>ISO88592</p></li><li><p>ISO88595</p></li><li><p>ISO88596</p></li><li><p>ISO88597</p></li><li><p>ISO88598</p></li><li><p>ISO88599</p></li><li><p>KOI8R</p></li><li><p>SHIFTJIS</p></li><li><p>UTF8</p></li><li><p>UTF16</p></li><li><p>UTF16BE</p></li><li><p>UTF16LE</p></li><li><p>UTF32</p></li><li><p>UTF32BE</p></li><li><p>UTF32LE</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1250</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1251</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1252</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1253</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1254</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1255</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1256</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>UTF8</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### File Format Options JSON

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>GZIP</p></li><li><p>BZ2</p></li><li><p>BROTLI</p></li><li><p>ZSTD</p></li><li><p>DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>RAW_DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Date Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Binary Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HEX</p></li><li><p>BASE64</p></li><li><p>UTF8</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HEX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trim Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first value to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Extension</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enable Octal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Allow Duplicate</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Strip Outer Array</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Strip Null Values</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies that the JSON parser should remove object fields or array elements containing null values.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replace Invalid Characters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to replace invalid UTF-8 characters with a default Unicode character instead of resulting in an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ignore UTF8 Errors</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to suppress UTF-8 encoding errors and silently replace with a default Unicode character.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Skip Byte Order Mark</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to skip any byte order mark information from input files so that they wonât cause errors or be merged into the first table column.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### ORC

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Trim Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first value to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parquet

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>LZO</p></li><li><p>SNAPPY</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Binary As Text</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to interpret columns with no defined logical data type as UTF-8 text when set to true, or binary data when set to false.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trim Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first value to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### XML

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>GZIP</p></li><li><p>BZ2</p></li><li><p>BROTLI</p></li><li><p>ZSTD</p></li><li><p>DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>RAW_DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ignore UTF8 Errors</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to suppress UTF-8 encoding errors and silently replace with a default Unicode character.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Preserve Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the XML parser preserves leading and trailing spaces in element content.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Strip Outer Element</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the XML parser strips out the outer XML element, exposing second-level elements as separate documents.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Snowflake Data</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the XML parser disables recognition of Snowflake semi-structured data tags.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Auto Convert</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the XML parser disables automatic conversion of numeric and Boolean values from text to native representation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Skip Byte Order Mark</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to skip any byte order mark information from input files so that they wonât cause errors or be merged into the first table column.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first value to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### File Format Definition By Name

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>File Format Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies an existing named file format to use for loading and unloading data into or from the table. The named file format determines the format type (CSV, JSON, PARQUET), as well as any other format options, for the data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### File Format Definition By Type Options

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Format Type Options</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Avro">Avro</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#FileFormatOptionsCsv">File Format Options CSV</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#FileFormatOptionsJson">File Format Options JSON</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ORC">ORC</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Parquet">Parquet</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#XML">XML</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the type of files loaded and unloaded into or from the table.
