If a format type is specified, additional format-specific options can be specified.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### True

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Sns Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required only when configuring AUTO_INGEST for Amazon S3 stages using Amazon Simple Notification Service (SNS). Specifies the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the SNS topic for your S3 bucket. The CREATE PIPE statement subscribes the Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) queue to the specified SNS topic. The pipe copies files to the ingest queue triggered by event notifications via the SNS topic.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Integration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required only when configuring AUTO_INGEST for Google Cloud Storage or Microsoft Azure stages. Specifies the existing notification integration used to access the storage queue.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Validation Mode Return Errors

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Validation Error Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RETURN_ERRORS</p></li><li><p>RETURN_ALL_ERRORS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Validation Mode Return Max Rows

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Snowflake Location

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Stage Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Files are unloaded to the specified Snowflake location (internal/external stage, table, path):</p></div><div><ul><li><p>[namespace.]int_stage_name[/path] = Files are unloaded to the specified named internal stage.</p></li><li><p>[namespace.]ext_stage_name[/path] = Files are unloaded to the specified named external stage.</p></li><li><p>[namespace.]%table_name[/path] = Files are unloaded to the stage for the specified table.</p></li><li><p>~[/path] = Files are unloaded to the stage for the current user.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### External Location

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>External Location Provider</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#MicrosoftAzure">Microsoft Azure</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#GoogleCloudStorage">Google Cloud Storage</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#AmazonS3">Amazon S3</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>External system to integrate with Snowflake</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Internal Stage Location

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Stage Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Internal stage name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### External Stage Location

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Stage Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>External stage name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>External Location Provider</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#MicrosoftAzure">Microsoft Azure</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#GoogleCloudStorage">Google Cloud Storage</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#AmazonS3">Amazon S3</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>External system to integrate with Snowflake</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Microsoft Azure

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL for the Microsoft Azure.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Microsoft Azure Storage Integration Credentials</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#MicrosoftAzureStorageIntegration">Microsoft Azure Storage Integration</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#MicrosoftAzureKeyCredentials">Microsoft Azure Key Credentials</a></p></li><li><p>MicrosoftAzureNoCredentials</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Security credentials for connecting to Microsoft Azure.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encryption</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#AzureCse">Azure Cse</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required only for loading from or unloading into encrypted files; not required if storage location and files are unencrypted.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Google Cloud Storage

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL for the Google Cloud Storage.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Storage Integration Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the storage integration used to delegate authentication responsibility for external cloud storage.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encryption</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#GcsSseMks">Gcs Sse Mks</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required only for loading from/unloading into encrypted files; not required if storage location and files are unencrypted.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Amazon S3

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL for the Amazon S3.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Amazon S3 Storage Integration Credentials</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#AmazonS3StorageIntegration">Amazon S3 Storage Integration</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#AmazonS3KeyCredentials">Amazon S3 Key Credentials</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#AmazonS3RoleCredentials">Amazon S3 Role Credentials</a></p></li><li><p>AmazonS3NoCredentials</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The security credentials for connecting to AWS and accessing the private/protected S3 bucket where the files to load/unload are staged.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encryption</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#AwsCse">AWS CSE</a></p></li><li><p>AwsSseS3</p></li><li><p><a href="#AwsSseKms">Aws Sse Kms</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required only for loading from/unloading into encrypted files; not required if storage location and files are unencrypted.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### AWS CSE

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Master Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the client-side master key used to encrypt the files in the bucket. The master key must be a 128-bit or 256-bit key in Base64-encoded form. Client-side encryption (requires a MASTER_KEY value). Currently, the client-side master key you provide can only be a symmetric key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Aws Sse Kms

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Kms Key Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optionally specifies the ID for the AWS KMS-managed key used to encrypt files unloaded into the bucket. If no value is provided, your default KMS key ID is used to encrypt files on unload. Note that this value is ignored for data loading.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Gcs Sse Mks

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Kms Key Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key for Gcs Sse Mks encryption algorithm.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Microsoft Azure Key Credentials

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Azure Sas Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the SAS (shared access signature) token for connecting to Azure and accessing the private container where the files containing data are staged. Credentials are generated by Azure.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Table

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Table Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the name of the table from which data is unloaded.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Query

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Sql</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SELECT statement that returns data to be unloaded into files. You can limit the number of rows returned by specifying a LIMIT / FETCH clause in the query.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Validation Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Execute COPY in validation mode to return the result of a query and view the data that will be unloaded from the orderstiny table if COPY is executed in normal mode: copy into @my_stage from (select * from orderstiny limit 5) validation_mode='RETURN_ROWS';</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Definition By Name For Copy Into Location

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>File Format Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies an existing named file format to use for unloading data from the table. The named file format determines the format type (CSV, JSON, PARQUET), as well as any other format options, for the data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Definition By Type For Copy Into Location

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>File Format Options For Copy Into Location</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#CsvForCopyIntoLocation">Csv For Copy Into Location</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JsonForCopyIntoLocation">Json For Copy Into Location</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ParquetForCopyIntoLocation">Parquet For Copy Into Location</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the type of files unloaded from the table. If a format type is specified, additional format-specific options can be specified.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Csv For Copy Into Location

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>GZIP</p></li><li><p>BZ2</p></li><li><p>BROTLI</p></li><li><p>ZSTD</p></li><li><p>DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>RAW_DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Record Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more single-byte or multibyte characters that separate records in an input file or unloaded file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Field Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more single-byte or multibyte characters that separate fields in an input file or unloaded file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Extension</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String that specifies the extension for files unloaded to a stage. Accepts any extension. The user is responsible for specifying a valid file extension that can be read by the desired software or service.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Date Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Binary Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HEX</p></li><li><p>BASE64</p></li><li><p>UTF8</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HEX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Escape Character</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Escape Unenclosed Field Character</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Field Optionally Enclosed By</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first one to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Empty Field As Null</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Used in combination with emptyFieldAsNull when set as false allows empty strings to be loaded in tables without enclosing the field values in quotes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Json For Copy Into Location

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>GZIP</p></li><li><p>BZ2</p></li><li><p>BROTLI</p></li><li><p>ZSTD</p></li><li><p>DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>RAW_DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Extension</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parquet For Copy Into Location

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>LZO</p></li><li><p>SNAPPY</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Definition By Name For Copy Into Table

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>File Format Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies an existing named file format to use for loading data into the table. The named file format determines the format type (CSV, JSON, PARQUET), as well as any other format options, for the data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Definition By Type For Copy Into Table

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>File Format Options For Copy Into Table</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#AvroForCopyIntoTable">Avro For Copy Into Table</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CsvForCopyIntoTable">Csv For Copy Into Table</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JsonForCopyIntoTable">Json For Copy Into Table</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#OrcForCopyIntoTable">Orc For Copy Into Table</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ParquetForCopyIntoTable">Parquet For Copy Into Table</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#XmlForCopyIntoTable">Xml For Copy Into Table</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the type of files loaded into the table. If a format type is specified, additional format-specific options can be specified.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Avro For Copy Into Table

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>GZIP</p></li><li><p>BZ2</p></li><li><p>BROTLI</p></li><li><p>ZSTD</p></li><li><p>DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>RAW_DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trim Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first one to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Csv For Copy Into Table

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>GZIP</p></li><li><p>BZ2</p></li><li><p>BROTLI</p></li><li><p>ZSTD</p></li><li><p>DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>RAW_DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Record Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more single-byte or multibyte characters that separate records in an input file or unloaded file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Field Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more single-byte or multibyte characters that separate records in an input file or unloaded file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Skip Header Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Skip Blank Lines</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to skip any blank lines encountered in data, instead of resulting in an end-of-record error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Date Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Binary Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HEX</p></li><li><p>BASE64</p></li><li><p>UTF8</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HEX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Escape Character</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Escape Unenclosed Field Character</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a single character used as the escape character for unenclosed field values only.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trim Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Field Optionally Enclosed By</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first one to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error On Column Count Mismatch</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to generate a parsing error if the number of delimited columns (i.e. fields) in an input file does not match the number of columns in the corresponding table.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replace Invalid Characters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to replace invalid UTF-8 characters with a default Unicode character instead of resulting in an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Validate UTF8</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Empty Field As Null</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Used in combination with emptyFieldAsNull when set as false allows empty strings to be loaded in tables without enclosing the field values in quotes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Skip Byte Order Mark</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to skip any byte order mark information from input files so that they wouldnât cause errors or be merged into the first table column.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BIG5</p></li><li><p>EUCJP</p></li><li><p>EUCKR</p></li><li><p>GB18030</p></li><li><p>IBM420</p></li><li><p>IBM424</p></li><li><p>ISO2022CN</p></li><li><p>ISO2022JP</p></li><li><p>ISO2022KR</p></li><li><p>ISO88591</p></li><li><p>ISO88592</p></li><li><p>ISO88595</p></li><li><p>ISO88596</p></li><li><p>ISO88597</p></li><li><p>ISO88598</p></li><li><p>ISO88599</p></li><li><p>KOI8R</p></li><li><p>SHIFTJIS</p></li><li><p>UTF8</p></li><li><p>UTF16</p></li><li><p>UTF16BE</p></li><li><p>UTF16LE</p></li><li><p>UTF32</p></li><li><p>UTF32BE</p></li><li><p>UTF32LE</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1250</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1251</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1252</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1253</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1254</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1255</p></li><li><p>WINDOWS1256</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>UTF8</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Json For Copy Into Table

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>GZIP</p></li><li><p>BZ2</p></li><li><p>BROTLI</p></li><li><p>ZSTD</p></li><li><p>DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>RAW_DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Date Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Auto</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Binary Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HEX</p></li><li><p>BASE64</p></li><li><p>UTF8</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HEX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trim Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first one to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enable Octal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Allow Duplicate</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Strip Outer Array</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Strip Null Values</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies that the JSON parser should remove object fields or array elements containing null values.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ignore UTF8 Errors</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to suppress UTF-8 encoding errors and silently replace with a default Unicode character.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Skip Byte Order Mark</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to skip any byte order mark information from input files so that they wouldnât cause errors or be merged into the first table column.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Orc For Copy Into Table

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Trim Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first one to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parquet For Copy Into Table

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>SNAPPY</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Binary As Text</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to interpret columns with no defined logical data type as UTF-8 text when set to True, or binary data when set to False.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trim Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Null Ifs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a list of values from which Snowflake selects the first one to convert to from SQL NULL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Xml For Copy Into Table

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Compression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>GZIP</p></li><li><p>BZ2</p></li><li><p>BROTLI</p></li><li><p>ZSTD</p></li><li><p>DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>RAW_DEFLATE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a compression algorithm to use for compressing the unloaded data files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ignore UTF8 Errors</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to suppress UTF-8 encoding errors and silently replace with a default Unicode character.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Preserve Space</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the XML parser preserves leading and trailing spaces in element content.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Strip Outer Element</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the XML parser strips out the outer XML element, exposing 2nd level elements as separate documents.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Snowflake Data</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the XML parser disables recognition of Snowflake semi-structured data tags.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Auto Convert</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the XML parser disables automatic conversion of numeric and Boolean values from text to native representation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Skip Byte Order Mark</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to skip any byte order mark information from input files so that they wouldnât cause errors or be merged into the first table column.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Conditional

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Execution Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>FIRST</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Conditions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ConditionStatement">Condition Statement</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Else Into Clause</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Overwrite</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subquery</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Condition Statement

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>When Condition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Into Clauses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Unconditional

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Into Clauses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Overwrite</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subquery</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Not Matched Insert Clause

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Column Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Column Values</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Case Predicate</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Matched Update Clause

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Update Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Case Predicate</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Matched Delete Clause

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Case Predicate</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Snowflake Connector 1.4

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems in real time, as
described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Snowflake Connector Reference](snowflake-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Snowflake Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, type `snowflake` in the search field.
3.  Click **Snowflake Connector** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with Snowflake Connector:

- Snowflake > On Table Row  
  Initiates a flow by selecting from a table at a regular interval and generating one message per
  obtained row
- HTTP Listener  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- Scheduler  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an **On Table Row** source, follow these steps:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, select **Snowflake > On Table Row**.
2.  Drag **On Table Row** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **On Table Row** configuration screen, optionally change the value of **Display Name**.
4.  Click **+** next to **Connector configuration** to configure a global element that can be used
    by all instances of the **On Table Row** source in the app.
5.  On the **Snowflake Config** window, for **Required Libraries**, add the required Snowflake JDBC
    driver by selecting **Configure > Add recommended libraries** or **Configure > Add Maven
    dependency** and adding the artifact ID `snowflake-jdbc`.
6.  On the **Connection** section, specify the database connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify connection pooling and reconnection information,
    including a reconnection strategy.
8.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified Snowflake
    database.
9.  Click **OK** to close the window.
10. On the **On Table Row** configuration screen, in **Table**, select the name of the table to
    select from.
11. On the **On Table Row** configuration screen, in **Watermark Column**, select the name of the
    column to use for the watermark. Values taken from this column are used to filter the contents
    of the next poll, so that only rows with a later date than the watermark value are processed.
12. On the **On Table Row** configuration screen, in **Id Column**, select the name of the column to
    consider as the row ID. This parameter ensures the same row is not processed twice by concurrent
    polls.

For example, to configure an HTTP listener source, follow these steps:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of **Display Name**.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click **+** next to **Connector configuration** to configure a global element that can be used
    by all instances of the HTTP Listener source in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you are specifying an action for that connector to
perform.

To add an operation for Snowflake Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, select **Snowflake** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, configure a global element that all instances of that connector in
the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the authentication credentials
that the connector requires to access the target Snowflake system. Snowflake Connector supports the
Snowflake Connection type to connect to Snowflake.

To configure the global element for Snowflake Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click **+** next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  In the **General** tab, in **Required Libraries**, click **Configure** and select from the
    following options to install the required JDBC driver:
    - **Add recommended libraries**
    - **Use local file**
    - **Add Maven dependency**

4.  In **Connection**, enter the following required information to configure authentication:
    - **Account name**  
      Account name that was provided when registering for the Snowflake account.
    - **Warehouse**  
      Name of the virtual Snowflake warehouse to use. The specified warehouse must be an existing
      warehouse for which the specified account has privileges.
    - **Database**  
      Snowflake database to connect to. The specified database must be an existing database for
      which the specified account has privileges.
    - **Schema**  
      Snowflake database schema to use. The specified schema must be an existing schema for which
      the specified account has privileges.

      Either reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
      (recommended) or enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration properties.
      For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to configure them,
      see
      [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

5.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
6.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
7.  Click **OK**.

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for Snowflake Connector, configure the other required fields
for the connector. The required fields vary depending on which connector operation you use.

## View the App Log

To check for problems, view the app log:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the app log output is visible in the Anypoint
  Studio console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log output is visible in your
  OS console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`. You can configure the location of
the log path in the app log file `log4j2.xml`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Snowflake Connector Reference](snowflake-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Snowflake Connector 1.4 - XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio:

- If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML either from the Anypoint Studio XML
  editor or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
  statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.
- If you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
  connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/snowflake
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/snowflake/current/mule-snowflake.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the XML:

```xml
<mule xmlns:snowflake="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/snowflake"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/snowflake
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/snowflake/current/mule-snowflake.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
When you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access
to this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule4-snowflake-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `<version>` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `snowflake`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
